stimulus:
Welcome! In this study, you will play three short video games, completing a short survey after each game.

Please make sure you are in a quiet environment where you can focus for the duration of the study. Some games may use sound, so please ensure that your device's volume is not muted.

Press any key to begin.
Press any key to proceed to the first video game. Should you encounter a game-breaking bug, please report the issue in the Menu tab to the right of the screen.

You have completed your evaluation of the first video game.

Press any key to proceed to the second game.

response:
{"creativity-rating":4,"creativity-free-response":"The concept of a ball of light climbing platforms to escape darkness felt creative because it used a simple idea to represent something deeper like hope versus danger. The contrast between light and darkness gave the game a clear theme and emotional tone instead of just being a basic platformer. The visual concept of rising upward to escape added symbolism and made progression feel meaningful.","originality-free-response":"The theme of light escaping darkness felt meaningful and visually strong which added some originality to the experience. The aesthetic of a glowing ball moving upward through shadowy platforms created a clear atmosphere and gave the game a distinct mood. The upward progression symbolizing escape or survival also added a narrative layer beyond simple platforming.","originality-rating":5,"effectiveness-rating":4,"effectiveness-free-response":"The game was enjoyable because the simple controls and clear objective made it easy to understand while still being challenging. The contrast between the glowing ball and the surrounding darkness created tension and kept the experience engaging. The platforming felt functional and responsive which made successful jumps satisfying.","authenticity-rating":5,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":4,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"The game felt authentic because even though platforming is a common genre the light versus darkness theme gave it a genuine identity instead of feeling like a direct copy of another game. The visual style and focused concept showed that the developer had a clear idea they wanted to express.

In terms of intentionality the design choices such as the upward progression and the pressure from darkness felt purposeful rather than random. The difficulty curve and atmosphere suggested planning and thought behind the experience.

For effort the functionality and consistent theme showed solid work put into the mechanics and presentation. While the gameplay was somewhat simple it still demonstrated care in design and execution which made it feel thoughtfully developed.

"}

response: {"ai_blind_question":"No, I don’t think I would have answered the previous questions differently. My ratings and explanations were based on my actual experience of the gameplay, mechanics, and design rather than who created the game. Even knowing the games were AI-generated, the enjoyment, creativity, originality, and sense of effort I perceived would remain the same because those assessments reflect how the game felt to play, not the developer’s identity.\n\n"}
